description.task: Implement an ApiGateway class that routes incoming HTTP requests to downstream microservices based on URL path prefixes. The gateway should support route registration, request forwarding decisions, and path rewriting (stripping the prefix before forwarding).
instructions: addRoute() appends a Route to the routes list
resolve() finds the route whose prefix is the longest match against the start of requestPath
When stripPrefix=true, the rewrittenPath is requestPath with the prefix removed
When stripPrefix=false, the rewrittenPath is the original requestPath unchanged
resolve() returns null if no route prefix matches the start of requestPath
description.hint: Sort matching routes by prefix length descending to find the longest match first. Use requestPath.startsWith(route.prefix()) to check matches. For stripping: requestPath.substring(prefix.length()).

description.task: Build a RateLimiter class for an API gateway that limits the number of requests per client per time window. Use a fixed-window counter algorithm: each client gets a quota of N requests per window, resetting after the window expires.
instructions: clientState stores [requestCount, windowStartMs] for each client
allowRequest() fetches or initializes the client's state
If currentTimeMs >= windowStartMs + windowMs, reset the window: set requestCount=0 and windowStartMs=currentTimeMs
If requestCount < maxRequests, increment it and return true; otherwise return false
remainingQuota() returns maxRequests - requestCount after applying any window reset
description.hint: Use clientState.computeIfAbsent(clientId, k -> new long[]{0, currentTimeMs}) to initialize. After the window check and possible reset, test requestCount < maxRequests before incrementing.

description.task: Implement an AggregatorGateway class that implements the API Composition (Aggregator) pattern. When a client makes a single request, the gateway fetches data from multiple downstream services and merges the results into one response.
instructions: registerCall() appends a ServiceCall (key + fetcher supplier) to registeredCalls
aggregate() iterates over all registered calls and invokes each fetcher
Each call's returned map is placed into the result under its key
The aggregate result uses LinkedHashMap to preserve registration order
aggregate() should not throw exceptions from individual fetchers — wrap in try-catch and use an empty map for failed calls
description.hint: In aggregate(), for each call, do result.put(call.key(), call.fetcher().get()). Wrap in try-catch and put an empty Map.of() on failure. Use LinkedHashMap to preserve insertion order.
description.expectedOutput:
Keys: [user, orders, loyalty]
User name: Alice
Order count: 5
